roles.core: central bot, D1 authority, payment callbacks, report outbox and Durable Object live usage engine
roles.edge: reseller bots, customer miniapps and imported-subscription lifecycle
roles.processor: optional advanced schedules, backups, campaigns and background reports

core: LIVE_USAGE_COORDINATOR -> class LiveUsageCoordinator (required for cron-free live usage)

features: every new direct representative-panel usage delta is transactionally deducted from the owner wallet and recorded in wallet_ledger
central usage sync now reports both billed amount and actual wallet deduction
live usage reports separate direct billing, direct wallet collection, downline billing, downline collection, outstanding debt and total real collection
the central representative-traffic view displays owner wallet balances and total actual usage deductions
manual and background downline billing use the same idempotent event guard to prevent duplicate deductions
current-version-only update channel notes and the cleaned product picker from 3.2.3 are preserved
pending live-usage report rows from older versions are marked superseded during activation so stale zero-collection messages are not delivered later

deployment.migration_tag: v3.1.9-live-usage (unchanged; no new Durable Object migration)
deployment.automatic_provisioning: supported when cf_account_id, cf_api_token and cf_worker_name are already saved and the token can update Workers scripts
deployment.activation: deploy core, edge and processor; the existing LIVE_USAGE_COORDINATOR binding and alarm state are reused
deployment.cron: the existing 10-minute central cron can remain; it is not used for live usage while the Durable Object binding is active

validation.direct_usage_wallet_deduction: passed with SQLite first-charge and duplicate-event simulation
validation.downline_usage_idempotency: passed with SQLite collection and duplicate-event simulation
validation.live_report_collection_totals: passed; direct and downline collections are summed separately and as total real collection
